title: Q8 GGUF quantization — measured result on the single-seed judge, with foundation + same-engine bf16 arms

toolchain.quantizer: llama.cpp convert_hf_to_gguf.py --outtype q8_0 (Q8_0, direct, no f16 intermediate)
toolchain.server: llama.cpp llama-server, CUDA build (GB10 aarch64, CUDA 13), -ngl 999 --jinja
toolchain.eval_harness: scripts.sieve_capture_judge_http -> scripts/relabel/eval_relabel.py (same val set + labels as the bf16 vLLM measurement)
toolchain.serving_config_note: All three arms served identically (max_tokens 2048, temp 0.0, enable_thinking=False via chat_template_kwargs). Engine held constant, so the Q8-vs-bf16 delta isolates quantization.

foundation_base_bf16.note: 121/121 responses unparseable. The base has zero structured wp_judge capability; it cannot emit the rubric format at all. The entire judge skill is created by the fine-tune.

bf16_vllm_optimal_k.note: different engine (vLLM); absolute rho higher because vLLM run did not hit the same prose-truncation parse loss. Not the gate reference.

quantization_delta.interpretation: Q8 point estimate is 4.6pp below same-engine bf16, but the difference is smaller than the seed noise floor (0.052) and the two CIs overlap almost entirely ([0.593,0.821] vs [0.664,0.850]). Statistically indistinguishable at n~92.

collapse_check.verdict: Q8 does NOT collapse. Parse rate matches bf16 (76% vs 77%); rho within noise. This is the decisive contrast with uniform 4-bit nf4, which halved the parse rate and dropped rho to 0.165.

caveat.parse_fail_source: ~24% parse failures in BOTH judge arms come from the model's bimodal long-prose output truncated at max_tokens=2048, not from quantization. Applied equally to Q8 and bf16, so the delta is valid, but the absolute rho is depressed vs the vLLM figure. A confirmation run at max_tokens>=4096 (and/or the full 3-seed ensemble) would tighten the absolute numbers.
caveat.gate_status: MARGINAL vs the strict +/-2pp point gate (point delta -4.6pp exceeds 2pp), but WITHIN measurement noise. Q8 is coherent and non-collapsing.

verdict: Q8 GGUF SHIPPABLE with note. 47% smaller (56.8 -> 30.2 GiB), no collapse, judge rho within noise of bf16. Recommended ship tier for the single-seed judge. Foundation arm confirms the fine-tune created the entire judge capability (base: 0 parseable).
